prerequisites: A Pinterest business account, which is used to administer your app. You can log in with or create one, then verify your email and accept the Pinterest Developer Terms of Service.

A registered Pinterest developer app. Go to My apps, select Connect app, complete the request form, and submit for trial access review (reviewed each business day, with approval or denial sent by email).

App credentials issued after approval: your App ID and app secret key, both found on the My apps page under your app's details. These map to the credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret fields.
At least one registered redirect URI, configured under My apps > Manage > Configure > Redirect URIs, used during the OAuth authorization flow that produces the refresh token.

The OAuth scopes your sync requires (for example boards:read, pins:read, ads:read, user_accounts:read), granted when the user authorizes the app. Scopes follow the {entity}:read or {entity}:write syntax.
A start_date marking the earliest date from which to replicate data, plus any optional config such as account_id (the Pinterest ad account ID), status, custom_reports, and num_workers.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code grant with refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Pinterest authenticates via OAuth 2.0. Provide credentials.client_id (your App ID), credentials.client_secret (your app secret key), and credentials.refresh_token. Obtain the refresh token by running the Authorization Code grant: redirect the user to https://www.pinterest.com/oauth/ with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, and an optional state; receive the authorization code on your redirect URI; then POST it to https://api.pinterest.com/v5/oauth/token with grant_type=authorization_code, the code, and the matching redirect_uri, using HTTP Basic auth of base64(client_id:client_secret). The token response returns an access_token (prefixed pina), a refresh_token (prefixed pinr), and expiry values. pm uses the stored refresh_token to mint fresh access tokens automatically.

setupSteps.title: Create a Pinterest business account
setupSteps.body: Log in to or create a Pinterest business account. This is the account you will use to administer your developer app. Verify your email and accept the Pinterest Developer Terms of Service.

setupSteps.title: Register and get approval for a developer app
setupSteps.body: Go to My apps and select Connect app. Complete the request form with your app information and submit it for trial access. Applications are reviewed each business day, and you will receive an email when approved or denied.

setupSteps.title: Collect your App ID and app secret
setupSteps.body: After approval, open the My apps page and your app's details to find your App ID and app secret key. Use these as credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret. If the secret is ever compromised, reset it from My apps in the Configure tab via Reset app secret.

setupSteps.title: Register a redirect URI
setupSteps.body: In My apps, select Manage for your app, then on the Configure tab scroll to the Redirect URIs section and add at least one redirect URI. It must match exactly when you exchange the authorization code for tokens during the OAuth flow.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow: redirect the user to https://www.pinterest.com/oauth/ with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, and the scopes you need (for example boards:read, pins:read, ads:read, user_accounts:read). After the user approves, exchange the returned code at https://api.pinterest.com/v5/oauth/token (grant_type=authorization_code, using Basic auth of base64(client_id:client_secret)) to receive a refresh_token. Provide this as credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date to replicate from. Optionally set account_id (your Pinterest ad account ID), status, custom_reports, and num_workers as needed for your sync.
